rice-scoring.name: RICE Scoring
rice-scoring.description: Score features and opportunities by Reach, Impact, Confidence, and Effort to produce a ranked priority list.

origin.description: Developed at Intercom as a way to quantify feature prioritisation. Published as a blog post that became an industry standard.

slots.label: Items to score
slots.description: Features, opportunities, or solutions being evaluated

slots.label: Opportunities to score
slots.description: Opportunities scored on the same RICE Scoring inputs as features.

slots.label: Needs to score
slots.description: Needs scored on the same RICE Scoring inputs as features.

feature.label: Reach
feature.description: How many users will this impact per quarter?

feature.label: Impact
feature.description: How much will this impact each user, on the impact scale?

feature.label: Confidence
feature.description: How confident are you in the reach, impact, and effort estimates?

feature.label: Effort
feature.description: How much work is required to build and ship this, on the effort scale?

opportunity.label: Reach
opportunity.description: How many users will this impact per quarter?

opportunity.label: Impact
opportunity.description: How much will this impact each user, on the impact scale?

opportunity.label: Confidence
opportunity.description: How confident are you in the reach, impact, and effort estimates?

opportunity.label: Effort
opportunity.description: How much work is required to build and ship this, on the effort scale?

solution.label: Reach
solution.description: How many users will this impact per quarter?

solution.label: Impact
solution.description: How much will this impact each user, on the impact scale?

solution.label: Confidence
solution.description: How confident are you in the reach, impact, and effort estimates?

solution.label: Effort
solution.description: How much work is required to build and ship this, on the effort scale?

need.label: Reach
need.description: How many users will this impact per quarter?

need.label: Impact
need.description: How much will this impact each user, on the impact scale?

need.label: Confidence
need.description: How confident are you in the reach, impact, and effort estimates?

need.label: Effort
need.description: How much work is required to build and ship this, on the effort scale?

education.purpose: Quantify feature priority by scoring Reach, Impact, Confidence, and Effort so teams compare opportunities on the same scale instead of debating gut feelings.
education.core_question: Given limited engineering capacity, which features will deliver the most user value relative to the effort required?
when_to_use: You have more ideas or features than capacity to build them
Stakeholders disagree on what to build next
You need a transparent, defensible prioritisation process
when_not_to_use: You have a single obvious next step with no contention
The backlog is small enough to sequence intuitively

ice-scoring.name: ICE Scoring
ice-scoring.description: Rate ideas by Impact, Confidence, and Ease on a 1-10 scale. Multiply for a composite score. Fast and lightweight.

origin.description: Created by Sean Ellis as a lightweight growth experiment scoring method. Widely adopted in growth teams.

slots.label: Items to score
slots.description: Features or experiments being evaluated

slots.label: Impact
slots.description: How much will this move the needle?

slots.label: Confidence
slots.description: How sure are we about the impact?

slots.label: Ease
slots.description: How easy is this to implement?

slots.label: Opportunities to score
slots.description: Opportunities scored on the same ICE Scoring inputs as features.

slots.label: Needs to score
slots.description: Needs scored on the same ICE Scoring inputs as features.

feature.label: Impact
feature.description: Expected impact on the target metric (1-10)

opportunity.label: Impact
opportunity.description: Expected impact on the target metric (1-10)

need.label: Impact
need.description: Expected impact on the target metric (1-10)

education.purpose: Provide a lightweight scoring model for early-stage ideas when detailed effort estimates are unavailable. Faster than RICE and useful for brainstorm triage.
education.core_question: Which ideas should we investigate further based on their potential impact, confidence in our assumptions, and implementation ease?
when_to_use: You have more ideas or features than capacity to build them
Stakeholders disagree on what to build next
You need a transparent, defensible prioritisation process
when_not_to_use: You have a single obvious next step with no contention
The backlog is small enough to sequence intuitively

cost-of-delay.name: Cost of Delay
cost-of-delay.description: Quantify the economic cost of not shipping a feature to drive priority decisions. Combines urgency with value.

origin.description: Formalised in The Principles of Product Development Flow (Celeritas Publishing). Foundational to lean product economics.

slots.label: Items to evaluate
slots.description: Features or initiatives being assessed

slots.label: User-Business Value
slots.description: Revenue, retention, or strategic value

slots.label: Time Criticality
slots.description: How much value decays with delay

slots.label: Risk Reduction
slots.description: What risk does this mitigate?

slots.label: Opportunities to score
slots.description: Opportunities scored on the same Cost of Delay inputs as features.

feature.label: Cost of Delay
feature.description: Weekly revenue impact of not shipping

opportunity.label: Cost of Delay
opportunity.description: Weekly revenue impact of not shipping

education.purpose: Quantify the economic impact of not delivering a feature by a given date, making urgency visible and enabling time-sensitive prioritisation.
education.core_question: How much value are we losing every week this feature is not in production, and does that urgency justify fast-tracking it?
when_to_use: You have more ideas or features than capacity to build them
Stakeholders disagree on what to build next
You need a transparent, defensible prioritisation process
when_not_to_use: You have a single obvious next step with no contention
The backlog is small enough to sequence intuitively

wsjf.name: WSJF (Weighted Shortest Job First)
wsjf.description: Prioritise work by dividing Cost of Delay (user value + time criticality + risk reduction) by job duration to maximise economic throughput.

origin.description: Developed by Don Reinertsen and adopted as a core practice in the Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe). Combines urgency (Cost of Delay) with job size to produce an economic prioritisation sequence.

slots.label: Backlog Items
slots.description: Backlog Items: feature entries to evaluate

slots.label: User/Business Value
slots.description: User/Business Value: metric entries to evaluate

slots.label: Time Criticality
slots.description: How much value decays if delivery is delayed (deadlines, competition, seasonal windows)

slots.label: Risk Reduction / Opportunity Enablement
slots.description: Risk Reduction / Opportunity Enablement: metric entries to evaluate

slots.label: Job Size
slots.description: Estimated effort (story points, t-shirt size, or person-weeks)

slots.label: Opportunities to score
slots.description: Opportunities scored on the same WSJF (Weighted Shortest Job First) inputs as features.

feature.label: User/Business Value
feature.description: Relative value to users and the business if delivered

feature.label: Time Criticality
feature.description: How much value decays if delivery is delayed (deadlines, competition, seasonal windows)

feature.label: Risk Reduction / Opportunity Enablement
feature.description: Value from reducing risk or enabling future opportunities

feature.label: Job Size
feature.description: Estimated effort (story points, t-shirt size, or person-weeks)

opportunity.label: User/Business Value
opportunity.description: Relative value to users and the business if delivered

opportunity.label: Time Criticality
opportunity.description: How much value decays if delivery is delayed (deadlines, competition, seasonal windows)

opportunity.label: Risk Reduction / Opportunity Enablement
opportunity.description: Value from reducing risk or enabling future opportunities

opportunity.label: Job Size
opportunity.description: Estimated effort (story points, t-shirt size, or person-weeks)

education.purpose: Prioritise work by dividing the Cost of Delay by job duration, ensuring the most time-sensitive, valuable items are done first.
education.core_question: Considering the cost of waiting, which items should we start now to maximise economic benefit?
when_to_use: You need to coordinate work across multiple teams or time horizons
Stakeholders need visibility into what is coming and when
You want to balance commitments with flexibility
when_not_to_use: The team is small enough that informal coordination works
Plans would create false precision about uncertain outcomes

kano-model.name: Kano Model
kano-model.description: Classify features by how they affect user satisfaction: must-haves, performance features, and delighters.

origin.description: Created by Professor Noriaki Kano at Tokyo University of Science. Based on his theory of attractive quality.

slots.label: Must-haves
slots.description: Expected features: absence causes dissatisfaction

slots.label: Performance
slots.description: More is better: linear satisfaction increase

slots.label: Delighters
slots.description: Unexpected features: presence creates delight

slots.label: Indifferent
slots.description: Features users don't care about either way

feature.label: Functional Response
feature.description: How users feel when the feature IS present

feature.label: Dysfunctional Response
feature.description: How users feel when the feature IS NOT present

feature.label: Delighter classifications
feature.description: Count of survey responses classifying this feature as a delighter (attractive)

feature.label: Performance classifications
feature.description: Count of survey responses classifying this feature as performance (one-dimensional)

feature.label: Must-be classifications
feature.description: Count of survey responses classifying this feature as must-be (basic)

feature.label: Indifferent classifications
feature.description: Count of survey responses classifying this feature as indifferent

education.purpose: Classify features by how they affect user satisfaction (must-haves vs delighters). Teams invest in the right category at the right time.
education.core_question: Which features prevent dissatisfaction, which increase satisfaction linearly, and which create unexpected delight?
when_to_use: You need to distinguish must-have features from delighters
You want to identify features that drive satisfaction vs prevent dissatisfaction
You are investing in differentiation and need to know which delighters move users
when_not_to_use: You cannot survey users for functional/dysfunctional responses
The backlog is too early-stage for paired survey questions to be meaningful

raid-log.name: RAID Log
raid-log.description: A project management register tracking Risks, Assumptions, Issues, and Dependencies, the four categories most likely to derail a project if left unmanaged.

origin.description: Standard project management tool used across methodologies. The RAID log (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies) provides a single living document for tracking all factors that could derail a programme.

slots.label: Risk Register
slots.description: Risk or assumption logged with likelihood, impact, owner, and mitigation strategy

slots.label: Risk
slots.description: Individual risk scored by probability and impact; severity = probability * impact

slots.label: Dependency
slots.description: Dependency on another team, system, or deliverable that could block progress if not resolved

slots.label: Status Report
slots.description: Status update summarising RAID log changes: new risks, resolved issues, and dependency updates

slots.label: Deliverable
slots.description: Deliverable affected by logged risks, issues, or dependencies, linking RAID items to project scope

risk.label: Impact
risk.description: Consequence severity if the risk materialises (risk.impact assessment)

education.purpose: Track Risks, Assumptions, Issues, and Dependencies in a single living document so the project manager sees all potential blockers in one place.
education.core_question: What are the current risks, untested assumptions, open issues, and external dependencies that could derail this programme?
when_to_use: You are managing complex, multi-team initiatives with dependencies
You need to track risks, decisions, and milestones across workstreams
Stakeholders need visibility into programme-level progress
when_not_to_use: The project is small enough for a single team to manage
Formal programme management would create overhead without value
